biodegradable.disposal: Place in wet/green waste bin. Ideal for composting at home.
biodegradable.fun_fact: Biodegradable waste can be converted into compost that enriches soil and reduces the need for chemical fertilizers.

upcycling.title: 🌍 Home Compost
upcycling.desc: Layer with dry leaves and soil to make rich garden compost.

upcycling.title: 🌱 Seed Starter
upcycling.desc: Use cardboard/paper pieces as biodegradable seed starter pots.

upcycling.title: 🍵 Vegetable Stock
upcycling.desc: Boil vegetable peels with spices for free cooking stock.

upcycling.title: 🌻 Plant Fertilizer
upcycling.desc: Bury fruit peels near plants for a natural nutrient boost.

non_biodegradable.disposal: Separate by material (plastic/glass/metal). Place in dry recycling bin. Never burn.
non_biodegradable.fun_fact: Only 9% of all plastic ever produced has been recycled. The rest ends up in landfills or oceans.

upcycling.title: 🌿 Bottle Planter
upcycling.desc: Cut plastic bottles in half and use as planters for small herbs.

upcycling.title: 🐦 Bird Feeder
upcycling.desc: Cut holes in a plastic bottle, add a perch and fill with birdseed.

upcycling.title: 🕯️ Tin Lantern
upcycling.desc: Punch patterns into tin cans and place a tealight inside.

upcycling.title: 🎨 Upcycled Art
upcycling.desc: Use bottle caps, wrappers and cans to create mosaic wall art.
